description_long: This is a well-used baseball with a classic spherical shape, featuring a white surface mottled with grayish discoloration and visible signs of wear. The ball is encircled by two prominent, curved seams stitched with red thread, forming the traditional double-arch pattern characteristic of regulation baseballs. The surface texture appears slightly rough and matte, with scuff marks and dirt patches distributed unevenly across the exterior, suggesting extensive use in outdoor environments. No visible logos, manufacturer names, or league markings are present on any side of the ball. The stitching is tight and consistent, with each seam following a raised, zigzag path around the ball. This object is typical of what might be found in parks, streets, or neighborhood yards, reflecting its role as a piece of sports equipment in urban settings.
description: A worn, white baseball with red stitching, gray discoloration, and visible scuff marks. The surface is matte and rough, with no visible logos or markings.
description_view_0: This view shows the baseball's left seam prominently, with red stitching and a large patch of gray discoloration on the upper left.
description_view_1: The seam curves across the upper right, with a more even distribution of gray patches and less pronounced scuffing.
description_view_2: The front seam is visible, with a large, lighter area in the center and subtle gray mottling around the edges.
description_view_3: The seam arches across the upper left, with a darker gray patch on the lower right and consistent red stitching.
attribute_stitch_pattern: Two curved seams with red zigzag stitching, evenly spaced and forming a double-arch pattern typical of baseballs.
attribute_visible_wear_or_scuff_marks: Gray discoloration, dirt patches, and scuff marks are visible across the surface, especially on the upper left and lower right areas.
